rationale: Social Sciences at L3 splits each L2 (sociology, anthropology, political science, education, etc.) into its working sub-communities. Sociology gets 4 leaves (inequality/stratification, family, culture/religion, networks). Political science gets 4 leaves matching the APSA structure (comparative, IR, American politics, political behavior). Education research gets 4 leaves (K-12 + higher ed + teacher prep + education tech). Linguistics gets 4 leaves matching its theoretical/socio/typological/applied splits.

subfields.name: Inequality & Stratification
subfields.tagline: Social stratification, inequality and mobility, class.

uncovered_concerns: Did not split race/gender studies as their own L3 leaves — they live as research lenses applied across stratification, family, criminology, and labor at L3.
